etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 甫(甫) (fǔ) -> nourish; feed
etymology.interpretation: 哺 (bǔ, "nourish; feed") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating the act of feeding or nourishment through the mouth, with 甫(甫) (fǔ) serving as the phonetic component, providing the sound. The meaning relates to using the mouth to provide sustenance or nourishment, thus evolving the sense of 'feed.'

examples.chinese: 母鸟给小鸟哺食(母鳥給小鳥哺食)。
examples.english: The mother bird feeds the baby birds.

examples.chinese: 父母哺育孩子成长(父母哺育孩子成長)。
examples.english: Parents nourish and raise the children to grow.

examples.chinese: 奶牛哺乳的小牛非常可爱(奶牛哺乳的小牛非常可愛)。
examples.pinyin: Nǎiniú bǔrǔ de xiǎoniú fēicháng kě'ài.
examples.english: The calves being nursed by the cows are very cute.
